steps.title: Identify what the question requires
steps.content:
The question asks for the choice that emphasizes the different years the books reached the NYT Best Sellers list. The notes give two specific years: Secrets of a Sparrow in 1993 and The Crossover in 2017. The correct answer must put those years front and center as a contrast.
  • Choice A — Kwame Alexander and Diana Ross are both NYT best-selling authors.: Mentions neither year nor book title; the temporal difference is completely absent. ✗
  • Choice B — *The Crossover* and *Secrets of a Sparrow* are both books by Black authors that have reached the NYT Best Sellers list.: Names the books but omits the years entirely. ✗
  • Choice C — Kwame Alexander's *The Crossover* and Diana Ross's *Secrets of a Sparrow* are both NYT best sellers, but the former is a children's book, whereas the latter is a memoir.: Contrasts genre (children's book vs. memoir), not the years. ✗
  • Choice D — The memoir *Secrets of a Sparrow* became an NYT bestseller in 1993; *The Crossover*, a children's book, joined this list as well, but in 2017.: Explicitly names both years and frames them as a contrast with "but in 2017," making the temporal difference the focal point. ✓

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: Choice D — The memoir *Secrets of a Sparrow* became an NYT bestseller in 1993; *The Crossover*, a children's book, joined this list as well, but in 2017. is the only option that names both 1993 and 2017 and structures them as a contrast, directly emphasizing the different years. That matches Choice D — The memoir *Secrets of a Sparrow* became an NYT bestseller in 1993; *The Crossover*, a children's book, joined this list as well, but in 2017..
